@@ -208,7 +208,7 @@ {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fjw1","title":"Add basket item add/remove animations","description":"# Task: Animate Basket Item Add/Remove ## Context When items are added to or removed from the basket, they appear/disappear instantly. This feels abrupt compared to the smooth card animations elsewhere. Stripe/Linear use subtle animations for list changes. ## Current Behavior - Add item: Appears instantly at end of list - Remove item: Disappears instantly - Count badge: Updates without animation ## Proposed Solution ### 1. List Item Animation (BasketSidebar.tsx) ```typescript import { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion'; // Wrap basket items in AnimatePresence
{basketItems.map((item, index) => ( ))}
``` ### 2. Badge Count Animation (Nav.tsx or BasketSidebar.tsx) ```typescript // Animated counter for basket badge const [displayCount, setDisplayCount] = useState(count); const springCount = useSpring(count, { stiffness: 300, damping: 30 }); useEffect(() => { return springCount.onChange(latest => { setDisplayCount(Math.round(latest)); }); }, [springCount]); // Or simpler: scale animation on change {count} ``` ### 3. Add Success Feedback ```typescript // Flash effect when item added const [justAdded, setJustAdded] = useState(null); const handleAdd = (id: string) => { addToBasket(id); setJustAdded(id); setTimeout(() => setJustAdded(null), 1000); }; // In list: ``` ## Design Rationale - Slide in from left: Suggests item came from the content area - Slide out to right: Suggests item is being dismissed - Layout animation: Other items smoothly reposition - Stagger delay: Multiple adds feel organized, not chaotic - Badge pulse: Draws attention to count change ## Reduced Motion Support ```typescript const prefersReducedMotion = useReducedMotion(); const itemVariants = prefersReducedMotion ? { initial: {}, animate: {}, exit: {} } : { initial: { opacity: 0, x: -20 }, ... }; ``` ## Testing 1. Add item - slides in from left with fade 2. Remove item - slides out to right with fade 3. Add multiple - staggered entrance 4. Badge count - subtle pulse on change 5. Reduced motion - instant changes, no animation 6. Empty state transition - smooth crossfade ## Files Modified - apps/web/src/components/BasketSidebar.tsx - apps/web/src/components/Nav.tsx (badge animation) ## Dependencies - Framer Motion (already available) - Should be done after color consistency fixes","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:09:33.777021051-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T10:16:43.735469944-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T10:16:43.735469944-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented satisfying micro-interactions with spring animations, background flash, and haptic feedback","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fjw1","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-f3zc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-12T01:24:51.005541395-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fnf","title":"Initialize Next.js 16 app in apps/web -- Details: bun create next-app with TypeScript, Tailwind, ESLint, App Router, src dir. Add: framer-motion, lucide-react, clsx, tailwind-merge, class-variance-authority, zod, jszip, @radix-ui components. Dev deps: vitest, happy-dom, @testing-library/react, oxlint.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:27:08.324572997-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:23:42.257348127-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:23:42.257348127-05:00","close_reason":"Next.js 16 app initialized with React 19, Tailwind CSS 4, Turbopack, and workspace dependency on @jeffreysprompts/core. App builds successfully with static and dynamic routes.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fnf","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9ho","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:00.24920929-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fo8i","title":"Implement moderation appeal process","description":"## Origin Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-u8t6. ## Task Allow users to appeal moderation decisions. ## Why This Matters - Fairness to users - Catch false positives - Build trust - Legal protection ## Appeal Process ### 1. Submit Appeal - From suspension/removal notice - Explain why decision wrong - Provide evidence - One appeal per action ### 2. Review Queue - Admin reviews appeals - See original violation - See user explanation - Make decision ### 3. Outcomes - Upheld (keep action) - Overturned (reverse action) - Modified (reduce severity) ### 4. Communication - Notify user of outcome - Explain reasoning - Final decision (no re-appeal) ## Database ```typescript export const moderationAppeals = pgTable('moderation_appeals', { id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(), // What is being appealed moderationActionId: uuid('moderation_action_id').notNull(), actionType: text('action_type').notNull(), // content_removed, account_suspended, etc. // Appellant userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(), // Appeal content reason: text('reason').notNull(), evidence: text('evidence'), // Status status: text('status').default('pending'), // pending, reviewing, upheld, overturned, modified // Review reviewedBy: uuid('reviewed_by').references(() => userProfiles.id), reviewedAt: timestamp('reviewed_at'), reviewNotes: text('review_notes'), outcome: text('outcome'), createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(), }); ``` ## UI Components - AppealForm - Submit appeal - AppealStatus - Track appeal status - AppealQueue - Admin queue - AppealReview - Admin review panel ## API Endpoints - POST /api/appeals - Submit appeal - GET /api/appeals/[id] - Get appeal status - GET /api/admin/appeals - List appeals (admin) - PATCH /api/admin/appeals/[id] - Resolve appeal (admin) ## Limits - 1 appeal per moderation action - 7 days to submit appeal - Admin has 14 days to review - No re-appeals after decision ## Files to Create - src/lib/db/schema/appeals.ts - src/app/api/appeals/route.ts - src/app/(app)/appeals/[id]/page.tsx - src/app/(app)/admin/appeals/page.tsx - src/components/moderation/appeal-form.tsx ## Success Criteria - Appeals can be submitted - Admin can review efficiently - Users notified of outcomes - Process is fair and documented ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:23:45.794520237-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:23:45.794520237-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fo8i","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-p9c1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:35.935899026-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fuc1","title":"UX Flow Improvements: Filter discoverability and clarity","description":"# Epic: UX Flow Improvements ## Background & Problem Statement User testing and UX audit revealed several friction points in the main browsing experience: ### Issue 1: "Clear All Filters" Discoverability **Current state:** Plain text link that only appears when filters are active **Problem:** - Users don't notice it (blends with surrounding text) - No visual affordance before filters are applied - Uses indigo accent inconsistent with design system ### Issue 2: Featured Section Confusion **Current state:** Featured prompts appear before filter controls **Problem:** - Filters don't affect featured section (confusing) - No clear visual boundary between "featured" and "browse" modes - Users may think filtering is broken ### Issue 3: Basket Empty State **Current state:** Basic icon + text ("Your basket is empty") **Problem:** - Not engaging or helpful - No CTA to guide users - Missed opportunity for onboarding ### Issue 4: Active Filter Visibility **Current state:** Only text description below results header **Problem:** - Easy to miss that filters are active - No visual chips/badges for individual filters - Can't remove individual tags without going back to filter section ## Success Criteria - [ ] "Clear filters" is a visible button (ghost variant with X icon) - [ ] Featured section has clear visual separation - [ ] Basket empty state includes helpful CTA - [ ] Active filters shown as removable chips - [ ] All changes maintain Stripe/Linear aesthetic ## Design Principles - Make hidden things visible - Reduce cognitive load - Provide clear feedback - Guide users toward actions ## Dependencies - Should be done AFTER color consistency (Phase 1) - These are additive features, not breaking changes ## Estimated Scope - 3-4 files to modify - Medium complexity (some new components) - Requires design decisions on specifics","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:37:53.772613318-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T00:37:53.772613318-05:00"} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-fuc1","title":"UX Flow Improvements: Filter discoverability and clarity","description":"# Epic: UX Flow Improvements ## Background & Problem Statement User testing and UX audit revealed several friction points in the main browsing experience: ### Issue 1: "Clear All Filters" Discoverability **Current state:** Plain text link that only appears when filters are active **Problem:** - Users don't notice it (blends with surrounding text) - No visual affordance before filters are applied - Uses indigo accent inconsistent with design system ### Issue 2: Featured Section Confusion **Current state:** Featured prompts appear before filter controls **Problem:** - Filters don't affect featured section (confusing) - No clear visual boundary between "featured" and "browse" modes - Users may think filtering is broken ### Issue 3: Basket Empty State **Current state:** Basic icon + text ("Your basket is empty") **Problem:** - Not engaging or helpful - No CTA to guide users - Missed opportunity for onboarding ### Issue 4: Active Filter Visibility **Current state:** Only text description below results header **Problem:** - Easy to miss that filters are active - No visual chips/badges for individual filters - Can't remove individual tags without going back to filter section ## Success Criteria - [ ] "Clear filters" is a visible button (ghost variant with X icon) - [ ] Featured section has clear visual separation - [ ] Basket empty state includes helpful CTA - [ ] Active filters shown as removable chips - [ ] All changes maintain Stripe/Linear aesthetic ## Design Principles - Make hidden things visible - Reduce cognitive load - Provide clear feedback - Guide users toward actions ## Dependencies - Should be done AFTER color consistency (Phase 1) - These are additive features, not breaking changes ## Estimated Scope - 3-4 files to modify - Medium complexity (some new components) - Requires design decisions on specifics","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:37:53.772613318-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T17:59:12.972172782-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T17:59:12.972172782-05:00","close_reason":"All issues already resolved in previous work: (1) Clear Filters is a visible ghost button with X icon and badge count (page.tsx:231-251), (2) Featured section has visual separation with sparkles icon and gradient divider (FeaturedPromptsSection.tsx), (3) Basket empty state has illustrated icon, helpful text, and CTA button (BasketSidebar.tsx:219-252), (4) Active filters shown as removable chips (ActiveFilterChips.tsx)."} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-g0oe","title":"Fix PromptDetailModal.tsx: Replace zinc/indigo with neutral","description":"# Task: Unify colors in PromptDetailModal.tsx ## Context The prompt detail modal is a high-traffic component (every prompt view goes through it). It currently uses ~25 instances of zinc-* and 5+ instances of indigo-*, creating visual disconnect with the newly refactored cards and filters. ## Specific Changes Required ### Badge and header styling (lines 223-236) ```tsx // Before // After ``` ### Tag styling (lines 243-250) ```tsx // Before className="text-[11px] text-zinc-500 dark:text-zinc-500 bg-zinc-100 dark:bg-zinc-800/70 ..." // After (also fix text-[11px] → text-xs) className="text-xs text-neutral-500 dark:text-neutral-500 bg-neutral-100 dark:bg-neutral-800/70 ..." ``` ### Variables section gradient (lines 256-257) ```tsx // Before - Overly complex gradient that breaks Stripe aesthetic bg-gradient-to-b from-indigo-50/50 to-transparent dark:from-indigo-950/20 // After - Clean, subtle neutral styling bg-neutral-50/50 dark:bg-neutral-900/30 border border-neutral-200/50 dark:border-neutral-800/50 ``` Note: This is a design simplification, not just a color swap. ### Form input backgrounds (lines 276, 284) ```tsx // Before className="... bg-white dark:bg-zinc-900/50" // After className="... bg-white dark:bg-neutral-900/50" ``` ### Context textarea (line 308) ```tsx // Before className="... bg-zinc-50 dark:bg-zinc-900/50" // After className="... bg-neutral-50 dark:bg-neutral-900/50" ``` ### Prompt preview section (lines 315-320) ```tsx // Before border-zinc-200/80 dark:border-zinc-800/80 bg-zinc-50 dark:bg-zinc-900/50 // Fade gradient: from-zinc-50 dark:from-zinc-900/50 // After border-neutral-200/80 dark:border-neutral-800/80 bg-neutral-50 dark:bg-neutral-900/50 from-neutral-50 dark:from-neutral-900/50 ``` ### Button hover states (lines 386, 394) ```tsx // Before hover:bg-zinc-100 dark:hover:bg-zinc-800 // After hover:bg-neutral-100 dark:hover:bg-neutral-800 ``` ## Also Fix Typography - Line 233: `text-[11px]` → `text-xs` (token count) - Line 246: `text-[11px]` → `text-xs` (tags) - Line 288: `text-[11px]` → `text-xs` (variable description) ## Design Rationale - Modal should feel like a natural extension of the card that opened it - Variables section gradient was too "branded" - simplify for Stripe aesthetic - All form inputs should have consistent background treatment ## Testing 1. Open modal from PromptCard - verify visual continuity 2. Check all form inputs in dark mode 3. Verify variables section doesn't feel "flat" after simplification 4. Test with prompts that have many variables (grid layout) ## Dependency - Can be done in parallel with page.tsx changes - Should maintain consistency with PromptCard.tsx styling","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-12T00:35:27.872995897-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T02:20:52.404628783-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T02:20:52.404628783-05:00","close_reason":"All zinc/indigo colors already replaced with neutral. No text-[Npx] patterns found. Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Create comprehensive E2E tests for the command palette feature. ## Test Scenarios 1. **Open/Close** - Cmd+K opens palette - Escape closes palette - Click outside closes - Reopening preserves no state 2. **Search** - Type filters commands - Fuzzy matching works - No results message - Clear search button 3. **Navigation** - Arrow keys navigate - Enter executes command - Mouse click executes - Keyboard focus visible 4. **Commands** - Navigation commands work - Action commands execute - Recent commands appear first - Command categories display 5. **Accessibility** - Screen reader announces - Focus trap works - Role=dialog present ## Logging Requirements - Log keystroke to open/close - Log search queries - Log command selections - Screenshot palette states ## Files to Create - e2e/command-palette/open-close.spec.ts - e2e/command-palette/search.spec.ts - e2e/command-palette/commands.spec.ts - e2e/lib/command-palette-helpers.ts ## Success Criteria - All keyboard shortcuts work - Search is responsive - Commands execute correctly - Keep both repos in sync. ## Task Implement performance monitoring with focus on Core Web Vitals. ## Metrics to Track ### Core Web Vitals - LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) - target <2.5s - FID (First Input Delay) - target <100ms - CLS (Cumulative Layout Shift) - target <0.1 - INP (Interaction to Next Paint) - target <200ms ### Additional Metrics - TTFB (Time to First Byte) - FCP (First Contentful Paint) - TTI (Time to Interactive) - Bundle size per route ### Custom Metrics - API response times - Search latency - Time to first prompt displayed ## Implementation ### Client-Side - Use web-vitals library - Report to analytics endpoint - Real User Monitoring (RUM) ### Server-Side - Response time middleware - Database query timing - External API latency ### Reporting - Daily/weekly performance reports - Alerting on regression - Performance budget enforcement ## Tools Integration - Vercel Analytics (built-in) - Custom /api/vitals endpoint - Sentry performance monitoring ## Files to Create - src/lib/performance/web-vitals.ts - src/lib/performance/server-timing.ts - src/app/api/vitals/route.ts - src/components/performance-reporter.tsx ## Success Criteria - All Core Web Vitals green - Performance regressions detected - Actionable insights available ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:14.722910201-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:14.722910201-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xd0s","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-jaly","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:36.966369432-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xd0s","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-v6re","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-11T01:25:37.093516464-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xd0s","title":"Implement performance monitoring and Core Web Vitals","description":"## Origin Mirror from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-yfm. Static JSON files generated at build time - /registry.json - full prompt registry with all metadata - /registry.manifest.json - checksums for cache validation **/registry.json Format:** ```json { "schemaVersion": 1, "version": "1.0.0", "updatedAt": "2026-01-10T00:00:00Z", "prompts": [...], "bundles": [...], "workflows": [...], "meta": { "totalPromptCount": 50, "categories": ["ideation", ...], "tags": ["ultrathink", ...] } } ``` **/registry.manifest.json Format:** ```json { "version": "1.0.0", "generatedAt": "2026-01-10T00:00:00Z", "checksums": { "registry": "sha256:abc123...", "prompts": { "idea-wizard": "sha256:def456...", ... } } } ``` **Build Script Integration:** - scripts/build-data.ts generates both files - Files placed in apps/web/public/ - ETag headers based on manifest checksums - CLI uses manifest for cache validation **Location:** - apps/web/public/registry.json (generated) - apps/web/public/registry.manifest.json (generated) **Reasoning:** Static registry files enable CLI caching, offline PWA access, and efficient client-side search without API calls.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:34:33.677563084-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:39:27.800646247-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:39:27.800646247-05:00","close_reason":"Registry files already generated: registry.json and registry.manifest.json exist with proper schema version, checksums, and prompts data.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xgh","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-n3p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:57.215409515-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-xje","title":"Build PromptCard component -- Details: Displays prompt in grid with: category badge, title, description, tags (max 4 + overflow), preview expansion, copy button, add-to-basket button, difficulty indicator.
